text: Drove the openreview systematic-review engine end to end as a happy-path smoke test, standing up a fresh dozen-record pilot review (mouse-genetics-mesothelioma-mini) that reused the existing mouse-genetics-mesothelioma extraction_profile rather than the topic. After an early miscue where the broadest capped query (--retmax 12) returned recent clinical/omics papers instead of animal-genetics studies, the search was re-scoped to BAP1/asbestos-mesothelioma mouse work. Ran the full mechanics â€” capped PubMed search, dual-reviewer title/abstract screening, PMC full-text retrieval, full-text screening, and arm-level extraction on the two included studies (Bap1+/- germline mice, 38592450, showing increased susceptibility; Hmgb1 conditional knockouts, 37729199, mesothelial-specific KO protective). All validation and evaluation-gate checks passed and the review went live on the local dashboard at http://127.0.0.1:8765/ next to the original. At the user's request the pilot review was then deleted, leaving only the original example, with the workspace clean and validation still green.
